title: jasminocereus thouarsii subsp. howellii
intro: Jasminocereus thouarsii subsp. howellii, also known as the 'Desert Candle' or 'Howell's Night-blooming Cereus', is a cactus species native to the deserts of Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Jasminocereus thouarsii subsp. howellii include treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, ointments, or extracts, which can be used topically or ingested under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
